title: NPM tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: NPM Bearer token detected. Bearer tokens provide authentication for npm registry API requests. Exposing these tokens allows attackers to authenticate as you, access private packages, publish malicious packages, and compromise your npm account. Store tokens securely in environment variables and rotate immediately if exposed.

NPM registry token detected in variable assignment. Registry tokens provide authentication to npm registries for package operations. Exposing these tokens enables attackers to publish malicious packages, access private packages, modify package metadata, and launch supply chain attacks. Use environment variables or secure credential management.

NPM automation/CI token detected. Automation tokens for CI/CD pipelines provide privileged access to npm registries. Exposing these tokens allows attackers to compromise your build pipeline, publish malicious packages, access private packages, and inject malicious code into your software supply chain. Store in secure secret management systems.

NPM publish token detected. Publish tokens provide authorization to publish packages to npm registries. Exposing these tokens enables attackers to publish malicious versions of your packages, hijack package names, inject malware into the npm ecosystem, and compromise downstream users. Use read-only tokens when possible and store publish tokens securely.

Generic long authentication token detected in NPM context. Long alphanumeric tokens often represent authentication credentials for npm registries or custom registries. Exposing these tokens allows unauthorized access to npm operations, package publishing, private package downloads, and potential supply chain attacks. Verify token type and rotate immediately.
